topic: A person runs into their ex at a mutual friend's wedding
raw_output:
[story 1]

The music swelled, a vibrant, joyous wave washing over the ballroom. I clutched my glass of lukewarm champagne, trying to appear absorbed in the intricate floral arrangements lining the archway. Then, across the room, a familiar silhouette caught my eye. It was him. Liam. He was laughing, his arm draped around a woman whose dress shimmered under the chandeliers. My breath hitched. I immediately started fiddling with the stem of my glass, my fingers tightening around the crystal until my knuckles were white. Every muscle in my neck felt suddenly too tight, and I wished the floor would simply open up and swallow me whole. When his gaze swept across the room and snagged on mine, a hot flush crept up my throat, and I quickly looked down at my shoes, acutely aware of every misplaced strand of hair and the way my posture seemed to have crumpled inward.

[story 2]

I was strategically positioned near the buffet, pretending to be deeply interested in a platter of canapés. The air in the room felt thick, charged with forced merriment. Then, there he was. Liam. He moved with an easy confidence that always seemed to make me feel clumsy and oversized. Seeing him, I felt a distinct tightening in my chest, like a sudden constriction. I forced a smile, a brittle thing that didn't quite reach my eyes, and focused intently on selecting a cracker. When he finally navigated through the crowd and stopped near my section, the proximity was unbearable. I could feel the heat rising on my cheeks, a prickly sensation spreading across my skin. "Hey," he said, his voice warm. I managed a small nod, my throat suddenly dry, and I focused intensely on the pattern on the tablecloth, desperate to become invisible.

[story 3]

The noise level was intense, a symphony of clinking glasses and excited chatter. I was standing near the edge of the dance floor, nursing a drink, trying to look casual. And then I saw him. Liam. He was talking animatedly to someone, radiating an effortless charisma that made my own internal landscape feel suddenly muted and shaky. My gaze locked onto his, and a sudden, unwelcome wave of awkwardness washed over me. I shifted my weight from one foot to the other, an unconscious attempt to create distance. My hands, resting on the railing, began to tremble slightly. When he turned, and our eyes met for a fleeting second, I immediately dropped my gaze to the floor, my shoulders hunching up toward my ears. I felt every single nerve ending prickle with the urge to disappear into the wallpaper.
